scenario: A FEEL expression checks if a value score = 75 falls in the range "70 to 90 inclusive." The team wonders if FEEL has range / interval types and operators.
question: Does FEEL support range / interval types and operators?

options.text: FEEL's range type with bracket notation is part of the core FEEL language specification, which is shared between BPMN and DMN contexts in Camunda 8. 90] can appear in Gateway conditions, Input Mapping source expressions, timer duration calculations, and any other FEEL-evaluated context, not just in DMN table cells.

b] (open-closed). 90] true). 5]), etc. md:7-9, :22-87, :179-205. Multi-word names use spaces, NOT camelCase: met by (not metBy), overlaps before (not overlapsBefore), started by (not startedBy), finished by (not finishedBy).

Express range membership using a compound boolean expression like score >= 70 and score <= 90 -- this is a workable approach that is more verbose than necessary. 90]. Both approaches are logically equivalent, but the range notation is more readable, less prone to boundary errors, and makes the intent immediately clear. For half-open ranges, the bracket notation is significantly cleaner than compound inequalities.

FEEL has no range or interval type -- range-based conditions must be expressed through compound boolean inequality expressions -- this description does not reflect the actual behaviour. b]. The in operator tests membership in ranges. FEEL also provides 13 named range relation functions like before, after, meets, overlaps, finishes, starts, and coincides for temporal interval algebra.

a.text: Incorrect. FEEL's range / interval type is useful for compact boundary expressions: Notation: [a..b] (closed: both endpoints included), (a..b) (open: both excluded), [a..b) and (a..b] (half-open). For date / time ranges, use date / time values as endpoints: [date("2026-01-01")..date("2026-12-31")]. Membership operator in: this IS a true infix operator. =75 in [70..90] is true. Both point-in-range and range-in-range supported. Range relation FUNCTIONS (NOT operators) per feel-built-in-functions-range.md: every relation is a function call with 27 documented signatures total. Names with spaces, NOT camelCase: before(point1, point2) / before(range, point) / before(point, range) / before(range1, range2) per :22-87 after(...) (4 sigs) per :88-152 meets(range1, range2) per :154-177 met by(range1, range2) (two words) per :179-205 overlaps(range1, range2) per :206-232 overlaps before(range1, range2) (two words) per :233-259 overlaps after(range1, range2) (two words) per :260-286 finishes(...) (2 sigs) per :287-325 finished by(...) (2 sigs, two words) per :326-364 includes(...) (2 sigs) per :365-403 during(...) (2 sigs) per :404-442 starts(...) (2 sigs) per :443-481 started by(...) (2 sigs, two words) per :482-520 coincides(...) (2 sigs) per :521-559 Use cases: Date range validation: contract validity periods, age brackets. DMN unary tests: rule cells can use ranges directly (e.g., a rule matches if input is in [18..65]). Numeric bracket conditions: tax brackets, discount tiers.

b.text: Correct. FEEL is shared between BPMN and DMN; both support.

explanation: FEEL is shared between BPMN and DMN; both support.
